titleStmt.title: SII 40.45: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 45. (A.R. No. B. 24 of 1930.) Pulipāḍu, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a pillar set up in front of the Vēṇugōpalasvāmi temple in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1218, Durmukhi, Kārttīka ba. 15, Sunday, Solar eclipse, corresponding to 1296 A.D., October 28.

Records the gift of an oil-mill for the lamps in the temple of Chenna-Gōpīnātha at Pulipāḍu by Buruvumaḍla Malnāyaniṅgāru, for the merit of Guṇḍaya-Nāyaka and Māraya-Sāhiṇi, the favorite servants of the king, on the occasion of solar eclipse.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī śakavaruṣa
ṁbulu 1218 Agunēṭi
du¨rmmukhi saṁvatsara
kā¨rttika ba 15 Ānā
ṁṭi sū¨ryyagraha
ṇa kālamunapu
ḍu svasti śrī manmahā
maṁḍalēśvara kākatiyya pra
tāparudradeva mahārājulaku
niṣṭabhrityulaina guṁḍayanāya
ṁkulakūnu mārayasāhi
ṇiṁgārikini dha¨rmmā¨rtthamu
gānu būruvu maḍe malnā
yuniṁgāru pulipāṭice
ṁnnagōpīnāthani tiruvaḷikala dī
pānaku gānugu Ācaṁdra
kkamugānicciri . ravaruta
ṁpinisininavāri
laṁharibiyame . Below this inscription another inscription of 13th century A.D. runs as follows.
vibhava saṁvvatsara puṣya ba 10 bu nāṁḍu | guṁṇmagā-
ṭi dharmāyya
Digital edition of SII 40.45 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
